question: [File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Market settlement or maturity logic flaw that allows credit/debt units to redeem for more assets than economically owed'] [Function: mulDivDown / updatePositionView] Can an unprivileged lender, after N sequential updatePosition calls each rounding down the continuous-fee accrual via mulDivDown(postSlashPendingFee, accrualEnd - lastAccrual, maturity - lastAccrual), accumulate a credit balance strictly greater than credit - totalPendingFee_owed under PRECONDITIONS: market with nonzero continuousFee, lender with large credit and pendingFee, multiple partial accrual windows before maturity, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: take(buy_offer) -> warp(+dt) -> updatePosition -> warp(+dt) -> updatePosition -> ... -> withdraw(credit), violating INVARIANT: sum(withdrawn_assets) <= sum(repaid_debt_assets) + sum(sellerAssets_received), causing scoped impact: lender redeems more loan tokens than economically owed due to rounding-down fee undercharge? Proof idea: fuzz credit in [1, 2^128-1], continuousFee in [1, MAX_CONTINUOUS_FEE], ttm in [2, 360 days], N in [2, 100], assert sum(accruedFee_per_step) >= mulDivDown(pendingFee, ttm, ttm) and assert final_credit + sum(accruedFee) <= initial_credit.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Market settlement or maturity logic flaw that allows credit/debt units to redeem for more assets than economically owed'] [Function: mulDivDown / updatePositionView postSlashCredit] Can an unprivileged lender exploit mulDivDown rounding in postSlashCredit = credit.mulDivDown(type(uint128).max - lossFactor, type(uint128).max - lastLossFactor) to retain more credit than proportionally owed after a bad-debt realization under PRECONDITIONS: market with small totalUnits (e.g. 2), one unit of bad debt realized, lender with credit=1 and lastLossFactor=0, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: liquidate(seizedAssets=0, repaidUnits=0) to realize bad debt -> updatePosition(lender) -> withdraw(credit), violating INVARIANT: sum(lender_withdrawn) <= sum(repaid_debt) - badDebt_socialized, causing scoped impact: lender withdraws 1 token when economically owed 0 due to mulDivDown flooring the credit slash to zero? Proof idea: unit test with totalUnits=2, badDebt=1, credit=1, assert postSlashCredit == 0 after updatePosition.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Market settlement or maturity logic flaw that allows credit/debt units to redeem for more assets than economically owed'] [Function: mulDivDown / lossFactor update in liquidate] Can an unprivileged liquidator trigger repeated small bad-debt realizations where each lossFactor update uses mulDivDown((type(uint128).max - _lossFactor) * (_totalUnits - badDebt) / _totalUnits) to accumulate a lossFactor strictly less than the true proportional loss under PRECONDITIONS: market with large totalUnits, many small borrowers each with 1 unit of bad debt, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: for i in range(N): liquidate(borrower_i, seizedAssets=0, repaidUnits=0) -> updatePosition(lender) -> withdraw(all_credit), violating INVARIANT: loanToken.balanceOf(midnight) >= withdrawable after all withdrawals, causing scoped impact: lenders collectively withdraw more tokens than the contract holds due to lossFactor underestimation? Proof idea: fuzz N in [1, 1000], badDebt_per_step in [1, totalUnits/N], assert sum(postSlashCredit_i) <= totalUnits - sum(badDebt_i) after all liquidations.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Market settlement or maturity logic flaw that allows credit/debt units to redeem for more assets than economically owed'] [Function: zeroFloorSub / timeToMaturity in take] Can an unprivileged taker exploit zeroFloorSub(offer.market.maturity, block.timestamp) returning 0 at exactly block.timestamp == maturity to obtain a zero settlement fee on a take, then immediately withdraw the full credit at 1:1 face value under PRECONDITIONS: market at maturity boundary (block.timestamp == maturity), buy offer at tick=0 (price=WAD), nonzero settlementFeeCbp0, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: warp(maturity) -> take(buy_offer, tick=0, units=U) -> withdraw(U), violating INVARIANT: buyerAssets paid == sellerAssets received + settlementFee * U / WAD, causing scoped impact: buyer receives U credit units while paying only U * sellerPrice/WAD assets, effectively bypassing the settlement fee spread? Proof idea: unit test at block.timestamp == maturity, assert buyerAssets - sellerAssets == settlementFee(id, 0) * U / WAD.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Market settlement or maturity logic flaw that allows credit/debt units to redeem for more assets than economically owed'] [Function: mulDivDown / settlementFee interpolation integer division] Can an unprivileged taker exploit the integer division truncation in settlementFee = (feeLower * (end - timeToMaturity) + feeUpper * (timeToMaturity - start)) / (end - start) to obtain a settlement fee of 0 when feeLower and feeUpper are both set to CBP (minimum nonzero value) and units is 1, causing buyerAssets == sellerAssets under PRECONDITIONS: market with settlementFeeCbp0=1, settlementFeeCbp1=1 (fee = 1*CBP = 1e12), units=1, buyerPrice < 1e12, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: take(buy_offer, tick=MAX_TICK, units=1) -> withdraw(1), violating INVARIANT: claimableSettlementFee increases by at least 1 per take with nonzero fee, causing scoped impact: settlement fee is silently zero for dust-sized takes, allowing lender to redeem 1 credit unit for 1 loan token with no fee deducted? Proof idea: fuzz units in [1, 1000], settlementFeeCbp in [1, 14], assert claimableSettlementFee delta >= 0 and check when it equals 0.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Market settlement or maturity logic flaw that allows credit/debt units to redeem for more assets than economically owed'] [Function: mulDivUp / pendingFeeDecrease in withdraw] Can an unprivileged lender exploit mulDivUp(pendingFee, units, credit) rounding up the pendingFeeDecrease in withdraw to reduce their pendingFee faster than proportional, then after maturity call updatePosition to accrue a smaller-than-expected fee, resulting in a higher net credit than economically owed under PRECONDITIONS: lender with credit=C, pendingFee=P, partial withdraw of units=U where P*U is not divisible by C, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: withdraw(U) -> warp(maturity+1) -> updatePosition -> withdraw(remaining_credit), violating INVARIANT: total_withdrawn <= C - total_fee_owed, causing scoped impact: lender redeems more assets than net credit after fee deduction due to mulDivUp over-reducing pendingFee? Proof idea: fuzz C, P, U with P <= C and U < C, assert pendingFeeDecrease_withdraw + accruedFee_post_maturity == P.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Market settlement or maturity logic flaw that allows credit/debt units to redeem for more assets than economically owed'] [Function: min / accrualEnd in updatePositionView] Can an unprivileged lender exploit min(block.timestamp, market.maturity) returning market.maturity when block.timestamp > maturity to cause the full pendingFee to be accrued in one updatePosition call post-maturity, but if lastAccrual was set to a timestamp after maturity (e.g. via a post-maturity updatePosition), the condition _lastAccrual < market.maturity becomes false and fee=0, allowing the lender to avoid all remaining fee accrual under PRECONDITIONS: lender with nonzero pendingFee, lastAccrual set to maturity via a prior updatePosition at exactly maturity, block.timestamp > maturity, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: warp(maturity) -> updatePosition (sets lastAccrual=maturity, accrues full fee) -> warp(maturity+1) -> updatePosition (fee=0 since lastAccrual==maturity), violating INVARIANT: pendingFee is fully accrued by maturity, causing scoped impact: if lastAccrual is set to maturity in the first post-maturity call, subsequent calls correctly return fee=0, but verify no double-accrual path exists? Proof idea: unit test with lastAccrual=maturity-1, warp to maturity, updatePosition, assert pendingFee fully consumed.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Market settlement or maturity logic flaw that allows credit/debt units to redeem for more assets than economically owed'] [Function: mulDivDown / postSlashPendingFee in updatePositionView] Can an unprivileged lender exploit the mulDivUp rounding in postSlashPendingFee = pendingFee - pendingFee.mulDivUp(credit - postSlashCredit, credit) to have their pendingFee reduced by more than proportional during a bad-debt slash, resulting in a lower pendingFee relative to their slashed credit, so that the net credit (credit - pendingFee) after the slash is higher than the pre-slash net credit scaled by the loss factor under PRECONDITIONS: market with bad debt, lender with credit=C, pendingFee=P close to C, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: liquidate(bad_debt) -> updatePosition(lender) -> withdraw(all_credit), violating INVARIANT: net_credit_post_slash / net_credit_pre_slash <= (1 - lossFactor_delta), causing scoped impact: lender redeems more net assets than economically owed after bad debt socialization? Proof idea: fuzz C in [1, 2^128-1], P in [0, C], badDebt in [1, totalUnits], assert postSlashCredit - postSlashPendingFee <= (C - P) * (1 - lossFactorDelta).

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Market settlement or maturity logic flaw that allows credit/debt units to redeem for more assets than economically owed'] [Function: toUint128 / claimContinuousFee] Can an unprivileged fee claimer (or market creator who sets themselves as feeClaimer) call claimContinuousFee with amount > continuousFeeCredit to trigger an underflow in _marketState.continuousFeeCredit -= toUint128(amount), and if toUint128 does not revert (amount <= type(uint128).max), does the uint128 subtraction underflow wrap around under PRECONDITIONS: continuousFeeCredit=X, amount=X+1 (still fits uint128), triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: claimContinuousFee(market, X+1, receiver), violating INVARIANT: continuousFeeCredit >= 0 and withdrawable >= 0 after claim, causing scoped impact: continuousFeeCredit and withdrawable wrap to near-max uint128, allowing subsequent lender withdrawals to drain the contract? Proof idea: unit test with continuousFeeCredit=1, call claimContinuousFee(2), assert revert due to underflow in Solidity 0.8.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Market settlement or maturity logic flaw that allows credit/debt units to redeem for more assets than economically owed'] [Function: mulDivDown / buyerAssets and sellerAssets in take] Can an unprivileged taker taking a sell offer (offer.buy=false) exploit mulDivUp(units, buyerPrice, WAD) for buyerAssets and mulDivUp(units, sellerPrice, WAD) for sellerAssets to have buyerAssets - sellerAssets < settlementFee * units / WAD (i.e., the fee collected is less than the fee rate implies) under PRECONDITIONS: units=1, buyerPrice and sellerPrice both near WAD, settlementFee > 0, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: take(sell_offer, units=1) -> assert claimableSettlementFee delta, violating INVARIANT: claimableSettlementFee[loanToken] += buyerAssets - sellerAssets >= floor(units * settlementFee / WAD), causing scoped impact: settlement fee is systematically undercollected for unit-sized takes, allowing lenders to redeem credit at a higher effective price than the fee-adjusted offer price? Proof idea: fuzz units in [1, 100], tick in valid range, assert buyerAssets - sellerAssets >= mulDivDown(units, _settlementFee, WAD).

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Market settlement or maturity logic flaw that allows credit/debt units to redeem for more assets than economically owed'] [Function: msb / collateral bitmap iteration in liquidate] Can an unprivileged liquidator trigger a scenario where msb(bitmap) returns an incorrect bit index due to the clz opcode behavior on a bitmap with only bit 0 set (bitmap=1), causing the collateral at index 0 to be skipped in the maxDebt and badDebt computation under PRECONDITIONS: borrower with only collateral at index 0 activated (collateralBitmap=1), triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: supplyCollateral(index=0) -> take(sell_offer) -> liquidate(collateralIndex=0, seizedAssets=0, repaidUnits=0), violating INVARIANT: badDebt computation must include all activated collaterals, causing scoped impact: bad debt is over-realized (collateral value not subtracted from badDebt), causing lenders to lose more credit than economically justified? Proof idea: unit test with bitmap=1, assert msb(1)==0 and verify badDebt loop processes index 0.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Market settlement or maturity logic flaw that allows credit/debt units to redeem for more assets than economically owed'] [Function: mulDivDown / lossFactor update when totalUnits == badDebt] Can an unprivileged liquidator realize all remaining debt as bad debt (badDebt == totalUnits) causing the lossFactor update (type(uint128).max - _lossFactor).mulDivDown(0, totalUnits) = 0, setting lossFactor = type(uint128).max, and then verify that any lender who calls updatePosition after this gets postSlashCredit = 0 (since _lastLossFactor < type(uint128).max branch gives credit.mulDivDown(0, ...) = 0) under PRECONDITIONS: market with totalUnits=T, single borrower with debt=T, no collateral, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: liquidate(seizedAssets=0, repaidUnits=0) -> updatePosition(lender) -> withdraw(0), violating INVARIANT: if lossFactor==max, all lender credit must be zero and no withdrawal is possible, causing scoped impact: verify lender cannot withdraw any assets after total loss, or find a path where credit is nonzero post-max-lossFactor? Proof idea: unit test with totalUnits=1, badDebt=1, assert creditOf(lender)==0 after updatePosition.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Market settlement or maturity logic flaw that allows credit/debt units to redeem for more assets than economically owed'] [Function: zeroFloorSub / badDebt in liquidate] Can an unprivileged liquidator manipulate the badDebt computation badDebt = debt.zeroFloorSub(sum(collateral_i * price_i / ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE * WAD / maxLif_i)) by choosing a collateralIndex with high price such that the mulDivUp rounding in the badDebt subtraction overestimates collateral value, causing badDebt to be 0 when it should be positive, preventing bad debt realization and leaving lenders with inflated credit under PRECONDITIONS: borrower with debt=D, collateral value (mulDivUp) just barely >= D, actual collateral value (exact) < D, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: liquidate(seizedAssets=0, repaidUnits=0) -> assert lossFactor unchanged, violating INVARIANT: if true_collateral_value < debt, badDebt > 0 must be realized, causing scoped impact: bad debt is not socialized, lenders retain credit that exceeds recoverable assets? Proof idea: fuzz collateral, price, maxLif near the boundary where mulDivUp(collateral, price, ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE).mulDivUp(WAD, maxLif) == debt.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Market settlement or maturity logic flaw that allows credit/debt units to redeem for more assets than economically owed'] [Function: mulDivDown / continuousFeeCredit in _updatePosition] Can an unprivileged lender exploit the fact that continuousFeeCredit += toUint128(accruedFee) in _updatePosition, where accruedFee = mulDivDown(postSlashPendingFee, accrualEnd - lastAccrual, maturity - lastAccrual), to have the sum of all accrued fees across all lenders be strictly less than the sum of all pendingFees at maturity, creating a gap where lenders collectively withdraw more than (totalRepaid - continuousFeeCredit) under PRECONDITIONS: N lenders each with credit=1 and pendingFee=1, maturity=T, each calls updatePosition at different times, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: for each lender: warp(t_i) -> updatePosition(lender_i) -> withdraw(credit_i), violating INVARIANT: sum(withdrawn_i) + continuousFeeCredit == totalRepaid, causing scoped impact: rounding losses in fee accrual cause protocol insolvency by a small amount per lender? Proof idea: fuzz N in [1, 1000], assert sum(accruedFee_i) + sum(remaining_pendingFee_i) == sum(initial_pendingFee_i).

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Market settlement or maturity logic flaw that allows credit/debt units to redeem for more assets than economically owed'] [Function: mulDivDown / postSlashCredit with lastLossFactor == lossFactor] Can an unprivileged lender who has already called updatePosition (so lastLossFactor == marketLossFactor) call updatePosition again in the same block and receive postSlashCredit = credit.mulDivDown(type(uint128).max - lossFactor, type(uint128).max - lossFactor) = credit (no change), confirming idempotency, but verify that if lossFactor increases between two updatePosition calls in the same transaction via a reentrant liquidation callback, the second updatePosition correctly applies the new lossFactor under PRECONDITIONS: lender with lastLossFactor=L, market lossFactor=L, callback that calls liquidate to increase lossFactor to L', triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: updatePosition(lender) [in callback: liquidate -> lossFactor=L'] -> updatePosition(lender), violating INVARIANT: credit after second updatePosition == credit.mulDivDown(max-L', max-L), causing scoped impact: if reentrancy allows double-slash or no-slash, lender credit is incorrect? [File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Market settlement or maturity logic flaw that allows credit/debt units to redeem for more assets than economically owed'] [Function: atMostOneNonZero / liquidate input validation] Can an unprivileged liquidator pass both seizedAssets=0 and repaidUnits=0 to liquidate (which passes atMostOneNonZero check since both are zero) to realize bad debt without seizing any collateral or repaying any debt, and if the borrower has no collateral (collateralBitmap=0), does badDebt == debt causing lossFactor to jump to max, wiping all lender credit under PRECONDITIONS: borrower with debt=D, collateralBitmap=0, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: liquidate(seizedAssets=0, repaidUnits=0, postMaturityMode=true) -> updatePosition(all_lenders) -> withdraw(0), violating INVARIANT: lenders must receive at least the collateral value before bad debt is socialized, causing scoped impact: lenders lose all credit in a market where borrower had no collateral, which may be economically correct but verify no double-realization path? Proof idea: unit test with debt=D, no collateral, assert lossFactor after liquidation and lender credit after updatePosition.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Market settlement or maturity logic flaw that allows credit/debt units to redeem for more assets than economically owed'] [Function: mulDivDown / sellerAssets in take for buy offer] Can an unprivileged taker taking a buy offer (offer.buy=true) exploit sellerAssets = units.mulDivDown(sellerPrice, WAD) rounding down to receive fewer assets than the maker intended, while the maker's credit increases by exactly units, creating a scenario where the taker (seller) receives sellerAssets < units * sellerPrice / WAD but the credit accounting records units credit for the buyer under PRECONDITIONS: buy offer at tick T, units chosen such that units * sellerPrice % WAD != 0, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: take(buy_offer, units=U) -> seller receives sellerAssets -> buyer has U credit -> buyer calls withdraw(U), violating INVARIANT: sellerAssets_received + fee_paid == buyerAssets_paid, causing scoped impact: rounding causes buyer to redeem U credit for U tokens while seller received less than U * sellerPrice / WAD? Proof idea: fuzz units and tick, assert buyerAssets - sellerAssets == claimableSettlementFee_delta and sellerAssets == mulDivDown(units, sellerPrice, WAD).

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Market settlement or maturity logic flaw that allows credit/debt units to redeem for more assets than economically owed'] [Function: mulDivDown / fee accrual with lastAccrual=0 for fresh position] Can an unprivileged lender with a fresh position (lastAccrual=0, set at position creation time which is block.timestamp=T0) call updatePosition at time T1 > T0 and have the fee computed as mulDivDown(pendingFee, min(T1,maturity) - 0, maturity - 0) = mulDivDown(pendingFee, min(T1,maturity), maturity), which for T1 << maturity gives a very small fee, but verify that lastAccrual=0 is treated as the market creation time or position creation time, not as Unix epoch 0 under PRECONDITIONS: fresh lender position with lastAccrual=0 (never updated), market maturity=M, block.timestamp=T1, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: take(buy_offer) [sets lastAccrual=T1 via _updatePosition] -> warp(T2) -> updatePosition, violating INVARIANT: fee accrual starts from position creation, not from Unix epoch 0, causing scoped impact: if lastAccrual=0 is treated as epoch 0, fee accrual window is maturity-0 instead of maturity-T_creation, causing fee to be undercharged? Proof idea: unit test with lastAccrual=0, maturity=1000 days from epoch, T1=500 days, assert fee == mulDivDown(pendingFee, 500 days, 1000 days) not mulDivDown(pendingFee, 500 days, maturity_from_epoch).

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Market settlement or maturity logic flaw that allows credit/debt units to redeem for more assets than economically owed'] [Function: mulDivDown / continuousFeeCredit scaling after bad debt in liquidate] Can an unprivileged liquidator trigger bad debt realization that scales down continuousFeeCredit via mulDivDown(continuousFeeCredit, type(uint128).max - newLossFactor, type(uint128).max - oldLossFactor) to a value less than the actual fee owed to the protocol, then have the fee claimer claim the reduced continuousFeeCredit, leaving lenders able to withdraw more than (withdrawable - continuousFeeCredit) under PRECONDITIONS: market with large continuousFeeCredit=F, bad debt realized reducing lossFactor, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: liquidate(bad_debt) -> claimContinuousFee(F_scaled) -> lenders withdraw(all_credit), violating INVARIANT: loanToken.balanceOf(midnight) >= sum(lender_credit) after fee claim, causing scoped impact: fee scaling after bad debt underestimates the fee, allowing lenders to over-withdraw? Proof idea: fuzz continuousFeeCredit, badDebt, totalUnits, assert continuousFeeCredit_after * (totalUnits / (totalUnits - badDebt)) >= continuousFeeCredit_before.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Market settlement or maturity logic flaw that allows credit/debt units to redeem for more assets than economically owed'] [Function: clearBit / collateral bitmap in liquidate loop] Can an unprivileged liquidator trigger an infinite loop or incorrect collateral valuation in the liquidate bitmap loop if clearBit(bitmap, i) where i = msb(bitmap) fails to clear the correct bit, causing the same collateral to be counted twice in maxDebt and badDebt under PRECONDITIONS: borrower with collateralBitmap having multiple bits set, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: supplyCollateral(index=5) -> supplyCollateral(index=10) -> take(sell_offer) -> liquidate(seizedAssets=0, repaidUnits=0), violating INVARIANT: each collateral is counted exactly once in maxDebt and badDebt, causing scoped impact: double-counting collateral value in badDebt subtraction causes badDebt to be 0 when it should be positive, preventing bad debt socialization? [File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Market settlement or maturity logic flaw that allows credit/debt units to redeem for more assets than economically owed'] [Function: mulDivDown / lif computation in post-maturity liquidate] Can an unprivileged liquidator exploit the lif = min(maxLif, WAD + (maxLif - WAD) * (block.timestamp - maturity) / TIME_TO_MAX_LIF) computation (which uses plain integer division, not mulDivDown) to obtain lif=WAD at block.timestamp == maturity+1 when TIME_TO_MAX_LIF=900 and (maxLif-WAD)*1/900 rounds to 0, causing seizedAssets = repaidUnits.mulDivDown(WAD, WAD).mulDivDown(ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE, price) = repaidUnits * ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE / price, effectively seizing collateral at oracle price with no liquidation incentive under PRECONDITIONS: post-maturity market, block.timestamp = maturity+1, TIME_TO_MAX_LIF=900, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: warp(maturity+1) -> liquidate(repaidUnits=R, postMaturityMode=true), violating INVARIANT: lif >= WAD + (maxLif-WAD)/TIME_TO_MAX_LIF at t=maturity+1, causing scoped impact: liquidator seizes collateral at 1:1 oracle price with no incentive, but borrower loses collateral at unfavorable rate? [File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Market settlement or maturity logic flaw that allows credit/debt units to redeem for more assets than economically owed'] [Function: mulDivUp / repaidUnits from seizedAssets in liquidate] Can an unprivileged liquidator pass seizedAssets=S to liquidate and receive repaidUnits = seizedAssets.mulDivUp(liquidatedCollatPrice, ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE).mulDivUp(WAD, lif) which rounds up twice, causing repaidUnits to be 1 more than the exact value, and then the borrower's debt is reduced by repaidUnits while the liquidator only paid repaidUnits tokens, creating a 1-unit discrepancy under PRECONDITIONS: seizedAssets chosen such that seizedAssets * price % ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE != 0, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: liquidate(seizedAssets=S) -> assert borrower.debt reduction == repaidUnits, violating INVARIANT: repaidUnits * lif / WAD * ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE / price <= seizedAssets (liquidator pays fair value), causing scoped impact: liquidator pays 1 extra unit but seizes S collateral, or borrower's debt is reduced by 1 more than the collateral value justifies? Proof idea: fuzz seizedAssets, price, lif near rounding boundaries, assert repaidUnits <= ceil(seizedAssets * price / ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE / lif * WAD) + 1.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Market settlement or maturity logic flaw that allows credit/debt units to redeem for more assets than economically owed'] [Function: tExchange / liquidation lock in take callback] Can an unprivileged taker use a sell callback (onSell) that calls liquidate on the seller (who is locked during the callback) to verify that liquidationLocked returns true and liquidate reverts with NotLiquidatable, but then check if a nested take within the sell callback can unlock the seller prematurely by calling tExchange(LIQUIDATION_LOCK_SLOT, id, seller, false) via a crafted callback under PRECONDITIONS: seller is the taker in a sell offer, seller has debt, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: take(sell_offer, sellerCallback=MaliciousCallback) -> onSell calls liquidate(seller) -> assert revert, violating INVARIANT: seller cannot be liquidated during take callbacks, causing scoped impact: if lock can be bypassed, seller is liquidated mid-take while their position is partially updated? [File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Market settlement or maturity logic flaw that allows credit/debt units to redeem for more assets than economically owed'] [Function: mulDivDown / buyerCreditIncrease and sellerCreditDecrease in take] Can an unprivileged taker exploit the interaction between buyerCreditIncrease = zeroFloorSub(units, buyerPos.debt) and sellerCreditDecrease = min(units, sellerPos.credit) to create a scenario where totalUnits increases by buyerCreditIncrease - sellerCreditDecrease > 0 without a corresponding increase in withdrawable, causing the invariant totalUnits == sumDebt + withdrawable to be violated under PRECONDITIONS: buyer has existing debt=D, seller has existing credit=C, units=U where U > D and U > C, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: take(buy_offer, units=U) -> assert totalUnits == sumDebt + withdrawable, violating INVARIANT: totalUnits == sum(all_debt) + withdrawable at all times, causing scoped impact: accounting desync allows lenders to withdraw more than the contract holds? [File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Market settlement or maturity logic flaw that allows credit/debt units to redeem for more assets than economically owed'] [Function: mulDivDown / settlement fee at breakpoint boundaries] Can an unprivileged taker exploit the piecewise linear interpolation in settlementFee at the exact breakpoint timeToMaturity=1 day (which falls into the 1-7 day bucket with feeLower=cbp1, feeUpper=cbp2, start=1 day, end=7 day) to obtain a fee of cbp1 * CBP, and verify that this is consistent with the 0-1 day bucket at timeToMaturity=1 day (which would give feeLower=cbp0, feeUpper=cbp1, end=1 day, giving cbp1 * CBP), confirming no discontinuity, but check if timeToMaturity=0 (post-maturity) gives cbp0 * CBP which could be 0 if cbp0=0 under PRECONDITIONS: market with settlementFeeCbp0=0, block.timestamp >= maturity, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: warp(maturity) -> take(buy_offer, units=U) -> withdraw(U), violating INVARIANT: settlement fee >= 0 for all timeToMaturity, causing scoped impact: zero settlement fee post-maturity allows lender to redeem credit at full face value with no fee? Proof idea: unit test with cbp0=0, assert settlementFee(id, 0) == 0 and verify this is intended behavior.
